whatThisIs: Explain the Advance Beneficiary Notice of Noncoverage (CMS-R-131) - the OMB-approved Medicare form a provider gives a Medicare fee-for-service beneficiary before delivering a service Medicare is expected to deny. Sophie Well walks through each section of the form (services, reason Medicare may not pay, estimated cost, and the three option boxes) so the patient understands what they are agreeing to before signing.
whenToUse: Use this when a provider hands you an ABN and asks you to sign it, or when a biller is preparing one for a service that Medicare statutorily excludes or is likely to find not reasonable and necessary. The ABN is the patient's notice and choice mechanism; it does not change Medicare's coverage decision and it does not apply to Medicare Advantage plans (which have their own pre-service notification rules).
